title: Second All-Time in Assists — Chasing Stockton's Record
author: CP3 Legacy Staff
excerpt: Chris Paul sits at #2 on the all-time assists leaderboard. The math on catching Stockton.

body.text: Chris Paul is currently second on the NBA's all-time assists leaderboard with 12,709 career dimes. The only man ahead of him is John Stockton, who finished his 19-year Utah Jazz career with 15,806 — a record many believed unbreakable.

Paul trails Stockton by 3,097 assists. At his 2024-25 pace of approximately 7.4 assists per game over 82 games (607 assists per season), Paul would need roughly 5.1 more seasons to catch Stockton — putting him at age 45 in the 2029-30 season.

Realistic? Probably not. But second place alone is a remarkable achievement. Paul passed Jason Kidd (12,091) in March 2024. He passed Steve Nash (10,335) in 2022. He passed Mark Jackson (10,334) and Magic Johnson (10,141) years ago.
Why Stockton's Record May Stand Forever

Stockton played 1,504 games — an NBA record. He missed only 22 games in 19 seasons. Paul, despite a remarkable run of durability, has missed considerably more time to injury. Stockton also benefited from a 19-year partnership with Karl Malone, one of the most durable and productive scorers in NBA history.
Even if Paul never catches Stockton, his place at #2 — with the second-most assists in NBA history — cements his legacy as one of the greatest passers the game has ever seen.
